Is it correct to state "consistent thickness" if there’s variability observed in certain cases?

No—it would be inaccurate to assert “consistent” thickness if significant variability exists across individuals or pathologies. However, in normal health contexts—the primary focus of standard anatomical descriptions—the CDJ demonstrates remarkable uniformity within the 1–2 μm range. Thus, stating “consistent” refers specifically to physiologically healthy specimens where no disease processes alter morphology.
